groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Fill these first — enough for a solid draft.

groups.title: Article information
groups.description: Type, goals, length, tone, and publication context.

groups.title: Website & brand
groups.description: Where the article will live and how the site converts.

groups.title: Author & experts
groups.description: Only real people and credentials you can verify.

groups.title: Audience
groups.description: Who reads this, what they need, and what confuses them.

groups.title: Search intent
groups.description: What the reader expects after searching.

groups.title: Keywords & SEO inputs
groups.description: Use real keyword data only — never invent metrics.

groups.title: SERP & competition
groups.description: What already ranks and how you will differ.

groups.title: Sources
groups.description: Approved materials the model may cite.

groups.title: Research requirements
groups.description: What must be verified before publishing.

groups.title: Facts & claims
groups.description: Approved facts only — leave blank if unknown.

groups.title: Monetization & disclosure
groups.description: Affiliate, sponsor, and commercial relationships.

groups.title: Calls to action
groups.description: Primary and secondary next steps for the reader.

groups.title: High-stakes / YMYL
groups.description: Medical, legal, financial, or safety-sensitive topics.

groups.title: Visuals
groups.description: Images, charts, and licensing constraints.

groups.title: RTL & localization extras
groups.description: Right-to-left and locale-specific notes.

groups.title: Freshness & updates
groups.description: Review dates and changeable information.

groups.title: Canonical & syndication
groups.description: Duplicate pages and republishing plans.

groups.title: Structured data
groups.description: Schema recommendations only — not code unless requested.

groups.title: Performance metrics
groups.description: How you will measure success after publish.

variables.label: Topic
variables.placeholder: How solo founders build a weekly content system
variables.hint: The subject the article is about — the reader’s real problem or question.

help.what: Working topic for the article.
help.why: Anchors the whole piece so the model answers one clear subject.
help.example: How solo founders build a weekly content system without a marketing team
help.avoid: Vague topics like “content marketing” or multiple unrelated themes.

variables.label: Primary Keyword
variables.hint: Main keyword — use naturally, no stuffing.

help.what: Primary target keyword or phrase.
help.why: Guides title, H1, and topical focus without keyword stuffing.
help.avoid: Invented search volume or ranking claims.

variables.label: Audience
variables.placeholder: solo founders publishing weekly without a content team
variables.hint: Primary reader — be specific.

help.what: Primary audience description.
help.why: Tone, depth, and examples depend on who reads.
help.example: Solo SaaS founders who write their own marketing
help.avoid: Everyone / general audience without constraints.

variables.label: Language
variables.hint: Language of the article body.

help.what: Output language.
help.why: All copy and metadata should match this language.
help.avoid: Mixing languages unless localization is intentional.

variables.label: Article Type
variables.hint: Pick one confirmed format from the list.

help.what: The editorial format of the piece.
help.why: Structure and depth depend on type (how-to vs comparison vs news).
help.avoid: Changing type only because another format “ranks better.”

variables.label: Search Intent
variables.hint: Primary intent — do not force a sale on pure info queries.

help.what: Primary search intent.
help.why: Controls whether the article teaches, compares, or transacts.
help.avoid: Treating every query as a chance to sell.
